description_long: This electric drill features a robust, angular blue body with visible signs of wear and scratches, suggesting frequent use in urban environments. The main housing is rectangular with a slightly faceted top, and two vertical rods extend from the upper section, possibly for alignment or as part of a mounting system. The drill has two cylindrical handles on either side, colored in a matching blue with black accents, and the lower section includes two black cylindrical legs or supports. The head of the drill is boxy, with a red rectangular marking on the front, and the drill bit area appears to be empty, with no bit currently attached. The surface finish is matte and slightly rough, with visible scuffs and chipped paint. No visible power cord, battery pack, or ventilation slots are present, and the overall design is utilitarian and industrial.
description: A worn, blue electric drill with a boxy body, cylindrical handles, and no visible drill bit, showing signs of heavy use and a matte, scratched finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the octagonal top, a single handle, and the absence of a drill bit.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the rectangular head with a red marking, two vertical rods, and both cylindrical handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the symmetry and the other handle.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the head, the two rods, and the overall boxy structure.
